title: Patch<T> + ChatCreateInput design is correct and worth defending
description: The Patch<T> three-way enum (Keep/Set/Clear) correctly models JMAP patch semantics without requiring Option<Option<T>>. The Default=Keep means callers can use ..Default::default() for unset fields. The resolve_client_id helper cleanly handles ULID auto-generation without exposing lifetime machinery. The ChatCreateInput enum matches SpaceJoinInput's established pattern. A future contributor may suggest collapsing back to Option<Option<T>> or separate struct methods — both are inferior.
